fields.title: Memory tool registration
fields.description:
Create extensions/tau/src/memory/index.ts and extensions/tau/src/memory/schema.ts — tool registration.

schema.ts:
- TypeBox schema for memory tool parameters:
  - action: StringEnum(['add', 'replace', 'remove'])
  - target: StringEnum(['memory', 'user'])
  - content: Type.Optional(Type.String()) — required for add/replace
  - old_text: Type.Optional(Type.String()) — required for replace/remove

index.ts — initMemory(pi, runtime):
- Register 'memory' tool via pi.registerTool
- Tool name: 'memory', label: 'memory'
- Tool description with behavioral guidance (port from hermes):
  - WHEN TO SAVE: user corrections, preferences, env facts, conventions (proactively)
  - PRIORITY: user preferences > env facts > procedural knowledge
  - DO NOT save task progress/session outcomes/temporary TODO state
  - No read action — memory is already in the system prompt
  - TWO TARGETS: 'memory' for agent notes, 'user' for user profile
  - ACTIONS: add, replace (old_text identifies), remove (old_text identifies)
- Handler: dispatch to CuratedMemory service via runtime.runPromise
- On success: return short status (entry count, usage %). NOT full entries.
- On MemoryLimitExceeded: return error with current entries so agent can consolidate
- On other errors: return descriptive error message

Wire initMemory into app.ts setup sequence (after CuratedMemory service is available).

Reference: .reference/hermes-agent/tools/memory_tool.py (MEMORY_SCHEMA, memory_tool function)
fields.notes: Implemented memory tool registration and schema, wired initMemory into app startup, and returned compact JSON status on success plus limit errors with currentEntries for consolidation. npm run gate passes in extensions/tau/.
fields.close_reason: Implemented memory tool registration and runtime wiring; work already landed in 619d8f8.
